agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"client","to":"upload-service","status":"correct","issue":"Accepts uploads up to 50MB with format validation. Request ID propagated in structured logs."},{"from":"upload-service","to":"S3","status":"risk","issue":"S3 failures are not broken out from general request failures. If S3 has a regional degradation, the on-call engineer cannot distinguish S3-specific errors from validation failures or client errors without reading logs. This delays triage."},{"from":"monitoring","to":"upload-service","status":"risk","issue":"Health endpoint checks S3 connectivity, which is good. However, without a latency histogram, there is no way to alert on SLA breach (p99 < 10s) proactively. You will only know you violated SLA after customers complain."}],"contract_compliance":"File size limit (50MB) and format validation are present. SLA of 99% < 10s exists but is NOT measurable with current metrics instrumentation.","data_flow_integrity":"Request ID in structured logs enables tracing a single upload. No distributed tracing mentioned, acceptable if this is a single-hop service."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["Confirm that S3 error responses (status code, error code, request ID from AWS) are logged — AWS support requires their request ID for escalations","Confirm retry attempts are logged individually so you can distinguish 'succeeded on retry 2' from 'succeeded first try'"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["CRITICAL: No latency histogram — cannot measure p99 latency, cannot alert on SLA breach, cannot build SLA burn-rate alerts. Add upload_duration_seconds histogram with buckets tuned around the 10s SLA (e.g. 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 15, 30)","CRITICAL: No file size metric — cannot correlate large files with slow uploads, cannot detect abuse patterns. Add upload_file_size_bytes histogram.","IMPORTANT: S3 errors not broken out — add s3_errors_total with labels for error_type (timeout, 5xx, throttle). Without this, a spike in requests_failed_total triggers a page but the on-call cannot tell if it is S3, validation, or client error without grepping logs at 3am.","NICE-TO-HAVE: Add in-flight upload gauge (uploads_in_progress) to detect connection pooling or concurrency issues under load."]},"tracing":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["Request ID in logs is sufficient for a single-service architecture. If this fans out to other services, add OpenTelemetry trace propagation."]},"debugging_capability":"Structured JSON logging with request ID, file size, and duration is a solid foundation. The main debugging gap is operational: without S3 error breakdowns in metrics, the on-call must grep logs to determine root cause during an incident."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"S3 regional outage or sustained 500s","handling":"partial","recommendation":"2 retries will exhaust, then all uploads fail. Health check will eventually fail, removing from load balancer — good. But 30s timeout × 3 attempts = up to 90s client wait before failure. Consider reducing timeout to 8-10s (aligned with SLA) so clients fail fast. Also add a circuit breaker: if S3 error rate exceeds threshold, fail immediately rather than queueing 90s of hopeless attempts."},{"scenario":"Large file (50MB) on slow network consuming upload slot for extended time","handling":"partial","recommendation":"A 50MB upload on a slow connection can hold a connection open well beyond 10s without any server-side issue. Ensure there is a client-facing ingest timeout (not just S3 timeout) so slow clients cannot exhaust connection pool. Track uploads_in_progress gauge to detect this."},{"scenario":"S3 throttling (429/503 SlowDown)","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Retries help, but they should use exponential backoff with jitter. Confirm retry strategy is not fixed-interval — fixed retries against a throttling S3 will amplify the problem."},{"scenario":"Disk or memory exhaustion from buffering large files","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Clarify whether uploads are streamed to S3 or buffered in memory/disk first. If buffered: 50MB × N concurrent uploads = significant memory pressure. If 200 concurrent uploads buffer in memory, that is 10GB. Add resource limits and concurrent upload caps."},{"scenario":"Poison file causing repeated validation crashes","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"If a malformed file causes a panic or unhandled exception in validation, it could crash the process. Ensure validation errors are caught, not propagated as crashes. Monitor restart count."}],"retry_config":"2 retries on S3 failures — acceptable count. VERIFY: retries use exponential backoff with jitter, not fixed interval. Fixed-interval retries during S3 throttling will make the situation worse.","timeout_config":"30s on S3 upload calls is TOO HIGH relative to the 10s SLA. With retries, worst case is 90s (30s × 3 attempts). Recommend reducing to 8s so that even with retries, total time stays near 24s and most requests either succeed fast or fail fast within SLA window.","circuit_breakers":"MISSING. If S3 is down, every request will burn 90s of server resources before failing. A circuit breaker that opens after N consecutive S3 failures would shed load immediately and let the system recover faster.","graceful_degradation":"MISSING. No mention of what happens when S3 is unreachable beyond retries. Consider: (1) returning a clear 503 with Retry-After header, (2) optionally queuing uploads to a local buffer or dead-letter for async retry if business requirements allow."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. Acceptable for a stateless upload service. Verify that no database migrations or S3 bucket policy changes are coupled to this deploy.","monitoring_coverage":"INSUFFICIENT. Cannot measure or alert on the primary SLA (p99 < 10s) without a latency histogram. This is not a nice-to-have — it is the core business commitment. Shipping without it means you are flying blind on the one metric that matters.","on_call_documentation":"Not mentioned. Needs a runbook covering: (1) what to do when upload error rate spikes, (2) how to distinguish S3 errors from validation errors, (3) how to check S3 status, (4) how to drain or restart the service safely.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add Prometheus latency histogram (upload_duration_seconds)","Add Prometheus file size histogram (upload_file_size_bytes)","Add S3 error breakdown metric (s3_errors_total with error_type label)","Reduce S3 timeout from 30s to 8-10s","Add alerting rules for p99 latency > 10s and S3 error rate"],"infra_provisioning":["Grafana dashboard or equivalent for upload latency, error rates, file sizes, and S3 health","Alerting rules in Prometheus/Alertmanager for SLA breach"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database involved. Files stored in S3. Rollback is safe — previous image version handles same S3 bucket."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Cannot measure or alert on the primary SLA (99% of uploads < 10s)","reason":"No latency histogram metric exists. The single most important operational metric for this service is unmeasurable. Without it, SLA violations are invisible until customers report them. Adding upload_duration_seconds histogram is a MUST-HAVE before production.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"S3 timeout of 30s with 2 retries creates 90s worst-case latency, 9x above SLA","reason":"The timeout configuration is misaligned with the SLA. A single S3 timeout already exceeds the 10s SLA by 3x. With retries, a failing request ties up server resources for up to 90s. This needs to be reduced to 8-10s with a circuit breaker to prevent cascading failure under S3 degradation.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["The latency histogram gap is unambiguous — you cannot alert on an SLA you cannot measure","The timeout/SLA misalignment is arithmetic: 30s > 10s","Retry and circuit breaker recommendations are standard operational practice"],"low_confidence_areas":["Memory buffering concern depends on implementation details not provided (streaming vs buffering)","Retry backoff strategy is assumed to need verification — it may already use jitter","Concurrent upload limits may already be handled at the load balancer level"],"assumptions":["This is a single-service architecture (no downstream services beyond S3)","Uploads are synchronous (client waits for S3 confirmation)","No CDN or upload proxy sits in front of this service","Standard Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack"]}}
